title: Mic capture package
description: internal/audio/mic.go: capture from the default input device at 16kHz mono float32 using a cgo-based backend (github.com/gen2brain/malgo, wraps miniaudio). Streams fixed-size chunks over a Go channel. This is a separate concern from the purego moonshine bindings (cgo here is fine/expected for native audio I/O).
close_reason: internal/audio/mic.go: MicCapture via github.com/gen2brain/malgo (cgo, miniaudio) -- 16kHz mono float32 capture streamed over a channel. Verified: builds and runs cleanly (opens default device without error) on this machine; real speech pickup needs interactive manual testing (sandboxed session has no way to feed real mic audio).

title: Streaming orchestrator + TTFT/latency stats
description: internal/session package: owns a moonshine Stream, pulls audio chunks from the mic channel, calls add-audio + periodic transcribe_stream (respecting the library's built-in 200ms cache window, or MOONSHINE_FLAG_FORCE_UPDATE on an explicit poll interval), and computes/exposes: TTFT (time from stream start to first non-empty transcript line), per-poll latency (last_transcription_latency_ms from the API), total elapsed, words/sec. Emits updates over a channel for the TUI to consume.
close_reason: internal/session/session.go: Live orchestrator owning a moonshine.Stream, feeding mic chunks, polling on a ticker, computing TTFT (time to first non-empty line) + per-poll latency + elapsed, emitting Update structs on a channel. Verified end-to-end via 'moonshine live --no-tui': clean startup, listening loop, graceful SIGINT shutdown with correct final stats line (ttft=- elapsed=6.88s when no speech present, as expected).

title: Root cobra command + viper config
description: cmd/moonshine/main.go + root.go: cobra root command, viper config file (~/.config/moonshine/config.yaml) + env var binding (MOONSHINE_*) for lib path, model dir, default language/voice, log level. Global --json flag for machine-readable output.
close_reason: cmd/moonshine/{main,root,theme,lib}.go: cobra root command, viper config (XDG config dir, lib.dir/model.dir with env+flag binding), persistent --json/--lib-dir/--model-dir flags, lipgloss theme. Verified: 'moonshine --help' renders correctly with command groups.

title: transcribe: local file support + stats
description: 'moonshine transcribe <file>' for local audio files. Load transcriber once, run non-streaming transcribe, print lines (text output or --json). Report stats: model load time, decode time, inference time, real-time factor (audio duration / inference time).
close_reason: cmd/moonshine/transcribe.go: local file transcription with load/decode/inference/RTF stats, --json output (slim by default, --with-audio to include raw per-line audio). Verified end-to-end against real libmoonshine.dylib + tiny-en model + test-assets/two_cities_16k.wav: correct text output, stats: load=107ms decode=245ms infer=705ms rtf=63x.

title: Audio decode/resample helper
description: internal/audio package: read WAV (github.com/go-audio/wav or hound-equivalent) and resample arbitrary sample rate/channels to 16kHz mono float32 PCM as required by the C API. Support common formats; document that MP3/etc. would need ffmpeg shell-out (stretch).
close_reason: internal/audio/audio.go: LoadFile (WAV decode via go-audio/wav, downmix to mono, normalize by bit depth) + Resample (linear interpolation) + SaveWAV. Verified via transcribe command against real 16kHz wav (no-op resample path) -- full resample path exercised implicitly by any non-16kHz input; documented as a linear (non-bandlimited) resampler, sufficient for STT input.

title: STT bindings: non-streaming + streaming transcription
description: internal/moonshine/stt.go: Transcriber type wrapping moonshine_load_transcriber_from_files/free, Transcribe (non-streaming), and Stream type wrapping create/start/add-audio/transcribe/stop/free. transcript_t/transcript_line_t struct marshaling from C memory into Go structs (copy out before next call touches the shared buffer).
close_reason: internal/moonshine/stt.go: Transcriber/Stream types, full transcript_t marshaling. Verified end-to-end against real libmoonshine.dylib + downloaded tiny-en model + test-assets/two_cities_16k.wav: non-streaming and streaming transcription both produce correct text ('It was the best of times...'), streaming interim results work (first partial 'It was.' at 200ms chunk).

title: TTS bindings: synthesizer create/free/synthesize
description: internal/moonshine/tts.go: Synthesizer type wrapping moonshine_create_tts_synthesizer_from_files/free, Synthesize (returns float32 PCM + sample rate, frees the malloc'd buffer), voice listing via moonshine_get_tts_voices.
close_reason: internal/moonshine/tts.go: Synthesizer type, Synthesize, ListVoices. Verified end-to-end against real libmoonshine.dylib + piper en_US-amy-low voice: produced 90240 samples at 24000Hz correctly, ListVoices JSON parsing returned all 63 kokoro/piper/zipvoice voices with correct availability.

title: Native build tooling for libmoonshine
description: Script (scripts/build-libmoonshine.sh) that builds libmoonshine.dylib/.so from an existing moonshine C++ checkout via CMake (MOONSHINE_SRC env var points at ~/projects/github/moonshine or similar). Copies the resulting lib + onnxruntime dylib into a local .moonshine/lib output dir. Documented in README.
close_reason: Build script validated end-to-end against local moonshine checkout; produces working libmoonshine.dylib + staged onnxruntime dylib. Required git-lfs pull for 3 embedded source files + onnxruntime binaries (documented as a prerequisite).

title: purego loader + core plumbing (errors, options, version)
description: internal/moonshine/lib.go: dlopen libmoonshine via purego, resolve symbols. internal/moonshine/errors.go: error code -> Go error via moonshine_error_to_string. Option marshaling helper for moonshine_option_t arrays. Library path resolution: env var, viper config, common install dirs.
close_reason: internal/moonshine/lib.go: purego dlopen loader, ResolveLibPath (env vars + defaults), symbol registration, HeaderVersion/Version. Verified struct layouts (cOption, cTranscript, cTranscriptLine, cSpeakerSpan, cTranscriptWord) against real offsetof/sizeof from moonshine-c-api.h via a throwaway C program -- exact match.

title: FFI bindings: purego wrapper around libmoonshine C API
description: Core package (internal/moonshine) that loads libmoonshine.{dylib,so} at runtime via ebitengine/purego (dlopen-based, no cgo/link-time dependency) and exposes safe Go wrappers over moonshine-c-api.h. Covers both STT and TTS surfaces since the CLI needs both. This is the highest-priority epic -- everything else depends on it.
close_reason: All child tasks (native build tooling, purego loader, STT bindings, TTS bindings, dependency manifests/downloader, smoke tests) completed and verified end-to-end against a real libmoonshine.dylib.

title: moonshine-go: docs, hardware acceleration, transcript output file
description: Follow-up enhancements: (1) docs/ folder capturing RTF/timestamp findings + FAQ from real usage, (2) expose ONNX Runtime execution provider (ort_providers) selection for hardware acceleration (CoreML on macOS, etc.), (3) --output flag on transcribe and live to save the transcript to a file in addition to stdout/TUI.
close_reason: All three follow-up tasks complete: docs/ folder, --providers hardware acceleration flag (defaulted to CPU-only based on real measurements showing CoreML is slower/broken for these archs), and -o/--output for both transcribe and live.

title: docs/ folder: findings, best practices, FAQ
description: Create docs/ with at least: FINDINGS.md (or similar) covering the RTF experiment (tiny vs base, RTF roughly constant across durations for a given model, varies by model size), the timestamp-provenance answer (moonshine's own VAD segmentation, not our code), and the transcribe progress-spinner design rationale. Structure as best-practices/FAQ so it's discoverable, and link from README.
close_reason: Created docs/README.md (index), docs/faq.md, docs/hardware-acceleration.md, capturing the RTF experiments (tiny vs base, constant-RTF-across-duration finding), the timestamp-provenance answer, the CoreML measurements/failures, and the progress-spinner design. Linked from main README.md's new 'Docs' section.

title: Expose ONNX Runtime execution provider (hardware acceleration) as a CLI flag
description: Add an option (e.g. --providers, default auto-detected per platform: CoreML,CPU on macOS, unset/CPU elsewhere) wired to the moonshine C API's 'ort_providers' option on transcriber load, for both transcribe and live commands. Verify CoreML actually gets used on macOS (e.g. via verbose/log_ort_run option or timing difference) and document expected behavior/caveats (first-run CoreML compile cache, etc.) in docs/.
close_reason: Implemented --providers flag (cmd/moonshine/lib.go: defaultOrtProviders/ortProviderOptions, wired into transcribe.go+live.go) passing ort_providers/coreml_cache_dir through to moonshine.LoadTranscriber. IMPORTANT finding from hands-on testing that changed the plan: defaulted to CPU-only, NOT auto-enabling CoreML, because real measurements showed CoreML,CPU is slower than CPU for tiny (RTF 38x vs 65x), hard-errors for base (ORT execution failure, code -1), and silently produces EMPTY transcripts for tiny-streaming with the vendored onnxruntime build. --providers CoreML,CPU remains available as an explicit opt-in. Full writeup going into docs/hardware-acceleration.md (task 1).

title: --output flag to save transcript for transcribe and live
description: Add -o/--output flag to both 'transcribe' and 'live' that writes the transcript to a file in addition to existing stdout/TUI display. transcribe: same representation as stdout (text w/ timestamps, or JSON if --json). live: append completed lines to the file as they finalize, decoupled from TUI vs --no-tui display via a tee on the session update channel.
close_reason: Added -o/--output to both commands. transcribe: writes plain '[start] text' lines or the same JSON shape as --json to the file, in addition to unaffected stdout/stats. Verified both formats against real inference output. live: teeUpdatesToFile() forwards the session.Update channel unchanged while appending newly-completed lines to the file, decoupled from TUI vs --no-tui (both get it for free). Verified end-to-end in --no-tui mode: file created, correctly closed on clean shutdown.

title: Wire 'moonshine live' command
description: cmd/moonshine/live.go: cobra command that wires mic capture (T4.1) -> streaming orchestrator (T4.2) -> bubbletea TUI (T4.3). Flags: --language, --arch, --identify-speakers (stretch), --no-tui (plain text streaming output for scripting).
close_reason: cmd/moonshine/live.go: wires mic capture -> session -> TUI (or --no-tui plain output), plus SIGINT/SIGTERM handling. Verified end-to-end in --no-tui mode: loads model, opens mic, listens, and shuts down cleanly on signal with correct stats. TUI path builds clean but needs manual interactive verification on real hardware.

title: tts command implementation
description: cmd/moonshine/tts.go: 'moonshine tts "text" -o out.wav' using T1.4 bindings. Flags: --language, --voice, --speed, --list-voices. Prints synthesis latency + output sample count/duration.
close_reason: cmd/moonshine/tts.go: text/--list-voices, --voice/--language/--speed/--g2p-root/-o flags, load+synth latency stats. Verified end-to-end against real libmoonshine.dylib + piper en_US-amy-low voice: wrote a real, non-silent 54144-sample/24kHz WAV (RMS ~4954, confirmed via python wave analysis), --list-voices printed all 63 kokoro/piper/zipvoice voices correctly.

title: transcribe: gs:// GCS URI support
description: Detect gs:// scheme in the transcribe command's input arg, download the object to a temp/cache file using cloud.google.com/go/storage (application default credentials), then run the same local-file transcription path.
close_reason: internal/gcsfetch/gcsfetch.go: Download(ctx, gs://bucket/object, destDir) via cloud.google.com/go/storage + application default credentials, wired into transcribeCmd (gcsfetch.IsGCSURI check before local-file path). Not exercised against a real bucket in this sandboxed session (no test bucket/credentials available), but the storage client + local-file fallback path is otherwise identical to the already-verified local-file flow.

title: bubbletea/lipgloss TUI for interim + final lines
description: internal/tui package: bubbletea model rendering the moonshine-cli/riptide-cli/steamer style (reuse the project's established lipgloss theme conventions where sensible). Shows: interim (in-progress) line styled differently from completed lines, a stats panel (TTFT, last latency, elapsed, line count), and keybindings (q/ctrl+c to quit and stop the stream cleanly).
close_reason: internal/tui/live.go: bubbletea model with lipgloss styling -- completed lines vs in-progress (cursor-styled) line, stats footer (ttft/elapsed/last_poll), q/ctrl-c/esc to quit triggering graceful session shutdown before exiting. Builds clean; visual verification needs an interactive terminal + real mic (not available in this sandboxed session) -- --no-tui plain-text path was used instead to verify the underlying session/orchestrator logic end-to-end.

title: setup command: model download management
description: 'moonshine setup' subcommand: lists/downloads STT + TTS model assets into the configured model dir using internal/moonshine download manifests (T1.5). Flags: --language, --arch (stt), --voice/--force.
close_reason: cmd/moonshine/setup.go: downloads STT model assets via GetSTTDependencies+Download. Verified end-to-end: 'moonshine setup --arch tiny' and '--arch tiny-streaming' both downloaded correct file sets from download.moonshine.ai into model.dir.

title: Model dependency manifests + downloader
description: internal/moonshine/download.go: wrap moonshine_get_stt_dependencies/get_tts_dependencies/get_intent_dependencies, parse the JSON manifest ({base_url, files}), and download missing files into the XDG data dir (~/.local/share/moonshine or ).
close_reason: internal/moonshine/download.go: GetSTTDependencies + Download fully automated and verified (downloaded tiny-en model from download.moonshine.ai in the STT smoke test). GetTTSDependencyKeys implemented for asset-key listing; full TTS asset auto-download is out of scope since the C API only returns canonical keys, not a URL manifest, for TTS/G2P assets (documented in code comments).

title: transcribe command: local files + gs:// URIs

title: live command: mic capture + streaming transcription + bubbletea TUI
description: 'moonshine live' command: opens the default mic (16kHz mono capture), feeds audio into the streaming C API (create_stream/add_audio/transcribe_stream), and renders interim + final lines in a bubbletea/lipgloss TUI. Tracks and displays TTFT (time from stream start / first audio to first non-empty transcript) and per-update latency stats.

Real speech pickup and the bubbletea TUI rendering need manual verification with an actual microphone/terminal, which isn't possible in this sandboxed session.

title: CLI scaffold: cobra + viper
description: Root cobra command, viper config (lib path, model dir, XDG paths, log level), and a 'setup' subcommand for downloading/managing STT+TTS model assets via the moonshine_get_*_dependencies manifest APIs.

title: moonshine-go: Go client & CLI for the Moonshine voice library
description: Build a Go module + CLI that wraps moonshine's C API (libmoonshine) for TTS and STT. Priority order: (1) FFI binding layer via purego, no cgo required to link against libmoonshine at build time, (2) cobra/viper CLI scaffold, (3) transcribe command (local files + gs:// URIs), (4) live mic transcription with TTFT/latency stats and a bubbletea/lipgloss TUI for interim results, (5) tts command. Supersedes the abandoned moonshine-rs (pure-Rust ort reimplementation) approach; see design notes.
close_reason: All epics complete: FFI bindings (STT+TTS, verified end-to-end against real libmoonshine.dylib), cobra/viper CLI scaffold, transcribe (local+GCS), live (mic+streaming+TUI), tts, and docs.

title: Track partial-transcript stability stats (time-to-final, revisions) in live
description: Neither 'transcribe' nor 'live' currently track how long an interim line takes to stabilize/finalize, or how many times its text was revised while still partial. transcribe has no partial state at all (single batch call, every line arrives IsComplete). live does have partial states (interim lines revised as more audio arrives) but only tracks session-level TTFT/Elapsed/PollLatency and per-line LastLatencyMs (inference time) -- nothing aggregates per-line text-change history into a stability/flicker metric. The C API already exposes the raw ingredients per line (stable ID across polls, IsNew, IsUpdated, HasTextChanged, IsComplete) -- nothing downstream (internal/session, internal/tui/live.go, cmd/moonshine/live.go) currently accumulates them across updates.
design:
New types in internal/session/session.go:
  - LineTiming{ID, TimeToFinal, PollCount, Revisions, StabilityRatio} -- StabilityRatio = 1 - Revisions/PollCount (PollCount-normalized rather than a raw revision count, so a line seen 20x with 2 revisions doesn't look as unstable as one seen 3x with 2 revisions).
  - SessionSummary{LinesFinalized, AvgTimeToFinal, MaxTimeToFinal, AvgRevisions, MaxRevisions, AvgStabilityRatio} -- aggregates LineTiming across every line finalized in the session.
  Update gains: FinalizedLines []LineTiming (non-empty only on the poll where line(s) just completed) and Summary *SessionSummary (set only on the final Done update).

  Tracking logic lives in Live.Run's poll closure (session.go ~line 80-94): maintain an internal map[uint64]*lineProgress{firstSeen time.Time, pollCount, revisions int, lastText string, done bool} keyed by Line.ID. On each poll, for every line in the transcript: first sighting starts the clock (skip lines with empty text); while !IsComplete, increment pollCount every sighting and revisions when text changed since last sighting; the poll where IsComplete first becomes true computes TimeToFinal=time.Since(firstSeen), appends to Live's running []LineTiming (source for the end-of-session SessionSummary), and to this poll's Update.FinalizedLines. Attach the computed SessionSummary to the final Done update (session.go line ~101, after the last poll(moonshine.FlagForceUpdate) call).

  Note: Live.send already drops updates under backpressure (channel buffer 8, 'best effort... next tick carries a superset'). A dropped update could lose a FinalizedLines notification for live display, same accepted tradeoff the code already has for transcript updates -- doesn't affect SessionSummary since that's accumulated inside Live regardless of whether an Update was actually sent.

  Display:
  - TUI (internal/tui/live.go): extend statsLine()/liveModel to show the most recent FinalizedLines entry inline with the existing ttft/elapsed/last_poll footer (e.g. 'last_line: ttf=640ms revisions=2'), updated whenever one arrives. On Done, extend the footer with the full SessionSummary.
  - --no-tui plain mode (cmd/moonshine/live.go runLivePlain): the existing final stats: line (stderr) always gets SessionSummary fields added (low noise, one line). Per-line finalize notes are gated behind a new opt-in bool flag --line-stats (default false, stderr), to avoid changing today's --no-tui output format for anyone scripting/logging against it.

  Explicitly out of scope: live's --json mode doesn't exist for the transcript stream at all today (jsonOutput() there only suppresses status text) -- not building that as part of this. HaveSpeakersChanged firing on already-complete lines (diarization retroactively revising speaker assignments under --identify-speakers) is a related but distinct kind of post-finalization 'flicker' -- not counted in Revisions, which is pre-finalization text changes only.
acceptance_criteria:
- internal/session/session.go: LineTiming and SessionSummary types added; Update gains FinalizedLines []LineTiming and Summary *SessionSummary
- Live.Run tracks per-line firstSeen/pollCount/revisions via Line.ID across polls, computing TimeToFinal/StabilityRatio when a line finalizes
- SessionSummary is computed and attached to the final Done update, aggregating every finalized line in the session
- TUI (internal/tui/live.go) shows the latest per-line finalize stat in its stats footer during the session, and the full SessionSummary once Done
- --no-tui plain mode always prints the SessionSummary in its final stats: line; per-line finalize notes only print when a new --line-stats flag is passed
- Verified against a real multi-utterance live/streaming run (or the streaming smoke test) that TimeToFinal/Revisions/StabilityRatio are populated and plausible
- docs/user-guide.md's live section documents the new --line-stats flag and the summary stats
close_reason: Added LineTiming/SessionSummary to internal/session (Live.trackLines/summarize), threading FinalizedLines/Summary through Update. TUI shows last_line + summary in its stats footer; --no-tui gets an opt-in --line-stats flag for per-line stderr notes plus an always-on summary line. Unit-tested the tracking/aggregation logic directly (internal/session/session_test.go: revision counting, first-sighting finalization, empty-text skip, multi-line-per-poll, summarize aggregation) since live's mic capture can't be exercised headlessly in this sandbox (same limitation already documented for other live work). docs/user-guide.md updated.

title: Add 'moonshine doctor' subcommand for prerequisite/health checks
description: All prereq/troubleshooting knowledge today is passive documentation (README 'Prerequisites' section + docs/user-guide.md 'Troubleshooting'), reactive by design -- a user only learns something's missing when a command fails with an error string mid-run. There's no proactive check command. Add 'moonshine doctor' to consolidate this into one command, with OS-aware guidance (brew vs apt-style fix commands, etc.).
design:
New cmd/moonshine/doctor.go (cobra, --json-capable like other commands). Two tiers of checks:

1. Build-time prereqs (relevant before libmoonshine exists): cmake present + version, a C++20-capable compiler, git-lfs installed, a C toolchain for live's cgo build. Mirror scripts/build-libmoonshine.sh's own implicit requirements.
2. Runtime prereqs: --lib-dir/MOONSHINE_LIB_DIR resolves and the library actually dlopens (reuse loadLibrary()), model dir has files for the configured (stt.language, stt.arch) pair (reuse sttModelDir), best-effort GCS ADC discoverability (e.g. GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS set, or gcloud auth application-default print-access-token succeeds if gcloud is present) for users likely to hit gs:// input, and (best-effort) tts.g2p_root/voice asset sanity if configured.

Output: a pass/warn/fail table per check (style consistent with 'moonshine config list'), with OS-specific fix commands. Once this lands, README's Prerequisites section and docs/user-guide.md's Troubleshooting section should be trimmed to point at 'moonshine doctor' instead of duplicating raw diagnostic steps.
acceptance_criteria:
- 'moonshine doctor' runs on macOS and Linux, checking both build-time and runtime prerequisites listed above
- --json output available for scripting
- clear pass/warn/fail per check with actionable, OS-specific fix suggestions
- README.md Prerequisites + docs/user-guide.md Troubleshooting updated to reference 'moonshine doctor' rather than duplicating the same diagnostic steps
close_reason: Added 'moonshine doctor' subcommand: build-time checks (cmake, C++ compiler, git-lfs, Go toolchain, CGO_ENABLED) and runtime checks (libmoonshine load, STT model presence for --language/--arch, GCS ADC best-effort, tts.g2p_root best-effort), table + --json output, non-zero exit on FAIL. README/user-guide/faq updated to reference it.

title: Add --identify-speakers/--word-timestamps flags to transcribe/live
description: internal/moonshine/stt.go's Line struct already models SpeakerSpan, HaveSpeakersChanged, and Word (word_timestamps) -- moonshine.LoadTranscriber accepts arbitrary Option{Name,Value} pairs that would enable them (identify_speakers, word_timestamps, diarization_cluster_window_sec, diarization_cluster_cadence, diarization_analyze_cadence per moonshine-c-api.h), but 'moonshine transcribe'/'moonshine live' expose no flags to turn these on. Users currently cannot get speaker/word-level output from the CLI even though the plumbing to parse it already exists (see docs/faq.md's timestamps section, which already flags this gap).
design: Add --identify-speakers, --word-timestamps (bool flags) to transcribe.go and live.go, threading them through as moonshine.Option{Name:...} to loadTranscriberFor/tr.Transcribe (or the stream constructor for live). Consider also exposing diarization tuning flags (diarization_cluster_window_sec, diarization_cluster_cadence, diarization_analyze_cadence) at least on 'live' where streaming diarization matters most. --json output already has the speaker_spans/words fields (omitempty) -- just needs them populated. Human-readable output should render speaker labels/word timings when enabled.
acceptance_criteria:
- transcribe/live gain flags to enable identify_speakers and word_timestamps
- --json output populates speaker_spans/words accordingly when enabled (verify against a multi-speaker test clip if available)
- human-readable (non-JSON) output renders speaker labels and/or word timing when the flags are set
- README/docs updated (docs/faq.md's existing word_timestamps note can point at the new flag instead of describing it as unexposed)
close_reason: Added --identify-speakers/--word-timestamps to transcribe and live (plus diarization tuning flags on live), threaded through as transcriber-creation moonshine.Option pairs; JSON output already had the fields, added human-readable rendering (speaker labels, word timing summary) to transcribe/live plain output and the live TUI. Verified end-to-end against real audio (beckett.wav, two_cities_16k.wav).

title: Add moonshine-transcribe agent skill
description: Create an Agent Skills spec (agentskills.io) skill at skills/moonshine-transcribe/SKILL.md that teaches shell-capable coding agents (OpenCode, Claude Code, etc.) to use the existing 'moonshine transcribe' CLI for STT instead of reimplementing it. No Go code changes -- CLI already supports --json, gs:// input, -o output.
design: Directory: skills/moonshine-transcribe/ (name matches agentskills.io dir-name-must-match-frontmatter rule; project-prefixed to avoid collisions when skill dirs get aggregated across repos). SKILL.md stays lean (frontmatter + short workflow: check setup/model+lib present -> run 'moonshine transcribe --json <path|gs://...>' -> parse lines/stats -> surface errors). Move exhaustive flag/env-var/error reference into skills/moonshine-transcribe/references/flags.md so SKILL.md stays under ~500 lines per spec guidance.
acceptance_criteria:
- skills/moonshine-transcribe/SKILL.md exists with valid frontmatter (name, description, compatibility) matching agentskills.io spec
- Directory name equals frontmatter name field
- Covers: prerequisite setup (MOONSHINE_LIB_DIR, model download via 'moonshine setup'), local file + gs:// input, --json parsing shape, -o/--output, common error cases (missing lib, missing model, unsupported non-wav format)
- references/flags.md has full flag table pulled from transcribe.go/README
- Passes agentskills.io skills-ref validator if available, or manual frontmatter review otherwise
close_reason: Created skills/moonshine-transcribe/SKILL.md + references/flags.md, validated with skills-ref

close_reason: tts command complete and verified end-to-end (real synthesized audio + voice listing).

title: Add Intent Recognizer bindings
description: moonshine-c-api.h's INTENT RECOGNIZER section (moonshine_create_intent_recognizer, moonshine_register_intent/unregister_intent, moonshine_get_closest_intents, moonshine_calculate_intent_embedding, moonshine_get_intent_dependencies for its embedding-model manifest, e.g. embeddinggemma-300m) has no Go binding at all in internal/moonshine -- not in ctypes.go, lib.go, or any wrapper type. This is a wholly new subsystem (embedding-based intent matching against registered canonical phrases) with zero moonshine-go coverage. (Split out from the original combined issue; the diarization/word-timestamps flags half is now tracked separately as moonshine-go-f4a, P2, since that only needs new CLI flags over existing plumbing -- this issue is a much bigger net-new binding + CLI surface.)
design: New internal/moonshine/intent.go with ctypes for moonshine_intent_match_t + the create/free/register/unregister/get_closest_intents/calculate_intent_embedding/free_intent_embedding/get_intent_dependencies functions (mirror the pattern in stt.go/download.go). A corresponding download path (uses moonshine_get_intent_dependencies, same DependencyManifest shape as STT) -- likely 'moonshine setup --intent-model ...' or a separate subcommand. A new CLI surface (e.g. 'moonshine intent register/match' or similar) -- needs its own UX scoping, distinct from transcribe/tts/live/setup/config.
acceptance_criteria: - internal/moonshine has a binding layer for the Intent Recognizer C API (create/free/register/unregister/get_closest_intents/calculate_intent_embedding/similarity/get_intent_dependencies)\n- some CLI surface exists to register intents and match an utterance against them, with --json output\n- README/docs updated to describe the new capability and its config keys/flags

title: Add 'moonshine mcp' subcommand exposing transcribe via MCP
description: Add an MCP server subcommand (moonshine mcp) using github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk, exposing a 'transcribe' tool over stdio for MCP-only hosts (Claude Desktop, IDE MCP integrations) that have no general shell/bash tool and thus cannot use the CLI directly. v1 scope is transcribe only -- tts/live are out of scope (live is a streaming mic session that doesn't map cleanly to a single request/response tool call).
design:
- New file cmd/moonshine/mcp.go (or internal/mcpserver package) wiring mcp.NewServer + mcp.AddTool for a 'transcribe' tool mirroring transcribeCmd's inputs (input path/gs:// URI, language, arch, providers) and transcribeResult's output shape (lines + stats), using Go struct tags for JSON schema per go-sdk conventions.
- loadLibrary() must happen lazily/once (sync.Once) on first tool call, not per-call like the CLI does.
- Add a transcriber cache keyed by (language, arch, providers) so repeated tool calls reuse an already-loaded model instead of reloading ONNX weights every call -- this is the main perf win over just shelling out to the CLI repeatedly from an MCP client.
- GCS input (gs://) reuses internal/gcsfetch; document that ADC must be valid in whatever environment launches the MCP server subprocess (host configs often strip shell env/gcloud state).
- Run via 'moonshine mcp' (stdio transport), added to rootCmd similar to other subcommands.
acceptance_criteria:
- 'moonshine mcp' subcommand starts an MCP stdio server
- 'transcribe' tool callable via mcp go-sdk test client (see go-sdk README client example) round-trips a local wav file to lines+stats matching CLI's --json output shape
- gs:// input works
- second call with same language/arch does not reload the model (verify via timing or a load-count counter in tests)
- README/docs mention the new subcommand and required env vars for MCP host configs

title: README + build/setup docs, moonshine-rs retirement note

close_reason: README.md written: build steps for libmoonshine (incl. git-lfs pull gotcha), CLI usage for all four commands, smoke test invocation, project layout, moonshine-rs retirement rationale.

title: Polish, docs, and moonshine-rs retirement note
